entries.name: android_aflags
entries.description: Android aconfig flags ("aflags") are a configuration system used to manage feature rollout and behavior across the Android platform
entries.fullDescription: Android aconfig flags ("aflags") are a configuration system used to manage feature rollout and behavior across the Android platform. For more details on aconfig, see: https://source.android.com/docs/setup/build/feature-flagging/declare-flag This view presents snapshots of the state of these flags as captured during the trace. Data is collected by the `android.aflags` data-source, which periodically polls the `aflags` tool on Android devices. Each row in this view represents the state of a single flag at a specific point in time (the `ts` column).

columns.name: ts
columns.description: Timestamp of the flag snapshot.

columns.name: package
columns.description: Package name of the flag (e.g. "com.android.window.flags").

columns.name: name
columns.description: Name of the flag within the package (e.g. "enable_multi_window").

columns.name: flag_namespace
columns.description: The namespace this flag belongs to (often used for grouping).

columns.name: container
columns.description: The container for the flag (e.g. "system", "product").

columns.name: value
columns.description: Current effective value of the flag at this timestamp.

columns.name: staged_value
columns.description: The value this flag will take after the next reboot, if a change has been staged.

columns.name: permission
columns.description: Whether the flag is read-only or read-write.

columns.name: value_picked_from
columns.description: Where the current value was picked from (e.g. "default", "local", "server").

columns.name: storage_backend
columns.description: The underlying storage backend for this flag (e.g. "aconfigd", "device_config").

columns.name: type
columns.description: Value type of the flag (e.g. "boolean", "integer").

entries.name: android_battery_stats_event_slices
entries.description: View of slices derived from battery_stats events
entries.fullDescription: View of slices derived from battery_stats events. Battery stats records all events as instants, however some may indicate whether something started or stopped with a '+' or '-' prefix. Events such as jobs, top apps, foreground apps or long wakes include these details and allow drawing slices between instant events found in a trace. For example, we may see an event like the following on 'battery_stats.top': -top=10215:"com.google.android.apps.nexuslauncher" This view will find the associated start ('+top') with the matching suffix (everything after the '=') to construct a slice. It computes the timestamp and duration from the events and extract the details as follows: track_name='battery_stats.top' str_value='com.google.android.apps.nexuslauncher' int_value=10215
entries.dataCheckSql: SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM counter_track WHERE name GLOB 'battery_stats.*' LIMIT 1) AS has_data

columns.name: value

entries.name: android_cpu_cluster_mapping
entries.description: Stores the mapping of a cpu to its cluster type - e.g
entries.fullDescription: Stores the mapping of a cpu to its cluster type - e.g. little, medium, big. This cluster type is determined by initially using cpu_capacity from sysfs and grouping clusters with identical capacities, ordered by size. In the case that capacities are not present, max frequency is used instead. If nothing is avaiable, NULL is returned.

columns.name: ucpu
columns.description: Alias of `cpu.ucpu`.

columns.name: cpu
columns.description: Alias of `cpu.cpu`.

columns.name: cluster_type
columns.description: The cluster type of the CPU.

entries.name: android_cpu_per_uid_counter
entries.description: View of counters for CPU-per-UID data
entries.fullDescription: View of counters for CPU-per-UID data. Each row represents one instant in time for one UID / cluster.
entries.dataCheckSql: SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM __intrinsic_android_cpu_per_uid_track LIMIT 1) AS has_data

columns.name: id
columns.description: ID for the row.

columns.name: ts
columns.description: Timestamp for the row.

columns.name: dur
columns.description: Time to the next measurement for the UID / cluster combination.

columns.name: track_id
columns.description: Associated track.

columns.name: diff_ms
columns.description: CPU time measurement for this time period (milliseconds).

columns.name: cpu_ratio
columns.description: Inferred CPU use value for the period where 1.0 means a single core at 100% utilisation.

entries.name: android_cpu_per_uid_track
entries.description: Table of tracks for CPU-per-UID data
entries.fullDescription: Table of tracks for CPU-per-UID data. Each row represents one UID / cluster combination.
entries.dataCheckSql: SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM __intrinsic_android_cpu_per_uid_track LIMIT 1) AS has_data

columns.name: id
columns.description: ID of the track; can be joined with cpu_per_uid_counter.

columns.name: uid
columns.description: UID doing the work.

columns.name: cluster
columns.description: Cluster ID for the track, starting from 0, typically with larger numbers meaning larger cores.

columns.name: total_cpu_millis
columns.description: Total number of cpu millis used by this track.

columns.name: package_name
columns.description: A package name for the UID. If there are multiple for a UID, one is chosen arbitrarily. UIDs below 10000 always have null package name.

entries.name: android_cpu_time_per_frame
entries.description: How much time did the frame take across the UI Thread + RenderThread
entries.fullDescription: How much time did the frame take across the UI Thread + RenderThread. Calculated as sum of `app VSYNC delay` `Choreographer#doFrame` slice duration and summed durations of all `DrawFrame` slices associated with this frame. Availability: from N (API 24). For Googlers: more details in go/android-performance-metrics-glossary.

columns.name: frame_id
columns.description: Frame id

columns.name: app_vsync_delay
columns.description: Difference between actual timeline of the frame and `Choreographer#doFrame`. See `android_app_vsync_delay_per_frame` table for more details.

columns.name: do_frame_dur
columns.description: Duration of `Choreographer#doFrame` slice.

columns.name: draw_frame_dur
columns.description: Duration of `DrawFrame` slice. Summed duration of all `DrawFrame` slices, if more than one. See `android_frames_draw_frame` for more details.

columns.name: cpu_time
columns.description: CPU time across the UI Thread + RenderThread.

columns.description: Virtual Set Size in kilobytes - total virtual memory mapped by the process.

columns.name: rss_kb
columns.description: Resident Set Size in kilobytes - actual physical memory used by the process.

columns.description: Proportional Set Size in kilobytes - amount of memory shared with other processes.

columns.name: anon_huge_pages_kb
columns.description: Anonymous huge pages in kilobytes - large anonymous memory regions.

columns.name: shmem_pmd_mapped_kb
columns.description: Shared Memory PMD mapped in kilobytes - page middle directory mapped shared memory.

columns.description: File PMD mapped in kilobytes - page middle directory mapped file memory.

columns.name: shared_huge_tlb_kb
columns.description: Shared huge TLB in kilobytes - shared huge page table entries.

columns.name: private_hugetlb_kb
columns.description: Private huge TLB in kilobytes - private huge page table entries.

columns.name: locked_kb
columns.description: Locked memory in kilobytes - memory that can't be swapped out.

columns.name: mapping_count
columns.description: Number of mappings of the object.

columns.name: mapped_object
columns.description: Path to the mapped object (file, library, etc.).

entries.name: android_frames
entries.description: Table based on the android_frames_layers table
entries.fullDescription: Table based on the android_frames_layers table. It aggregates time, duration and counts information across different layers for a given frame_id in a given process.
entries.dataCheckSql: SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM slice WHERE name GLOB 'Choreographer#doFrame*' OR name GLOB 'DrawFrame*' LIMIT 1) AS has_data

columns.name: frame_id
columns.description: Frame id.

columns.name: ts
columns.description: Timestamp of the frame. Start of the frame as defined by the start of "Choreographer#doFrame" slice and the same as the start of the frame in `actual_frame_timeline_slice if present.

columns.name: dur
columns.description: Duration of the frame, as defined by the duration of the corresponding `actual_frame_timeline_slice` or, if not present the time between the `ts` and the end of the final `DrawFrame`.

columns.name: do_frame_id
columns.description: `slice.id` of "Choreographer#doFrame" slice.

columns.name: draw_frame_id
columns.description: `slice.id` of "DrawFrame" slice. For now, we only support the first DrawFrame slice (due to b/384322064).

columns.name: actual_frame_timeline_id
columns.description: `slice.id` from `actual_frame_timeline_slice`

columns.name: expected_frame_timeline_id
columns.description: `slice.id` from `expected_frame_timeline_slice`

columns.name: render_thread_utid
columns.description: `utid` of the render thread.

columns.name: ui_thread_utid
columns.description: thread id of the UI thread.

columns.name: actual_frame_timeline_count
columns.description: Count of slices in `actual_frame_timeline_slice` related to this frame.

columns.name: expected_frame_timeline_count
columns.description: Count of slices in `expected_frame_timeline_slice` related to this frame.

columns.name: draw_frame_count
columns.description: Count of draw_frame associated to this frame.

columns.name: upid
columns.description: process id.

columns.name: process_name
columns.description: process name.

entries.name: android_frames_layers
entries.description: TODO(b/384322064) Match actual timeline slice with correct draw frame using layer name
entries.fullDescription: TODO(b/384322064) Match actual timeline slice with correct draw frame using layer name. All slices related to one frame. Aggregates `Choreographer#doFrame`, `actual_frame_timeline_slice` and `expected_frame_timeline_slice` slices. This table differs slightly from the android_frames table, as it captures the layer_id for each actual timeline slice too.
entries.dataCheckSql: SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM slice WHERE name GLOB 'Choreographer#doFrame*' OR name GLOB 'DrawFrame*' LIMIT 1) AS has_data

columns.name: frame_id

columns.name: dur
columns.description: Duration of the frame, as defined by the duration of the corresponding `actual_frame_timeline_slice` or, if not present the time between the `ts` and the end of the final `DrawFrame`.

columns.name: ts_end
columns.description: End timestamp of the frame. End of the frame as defined by the sum of start timestamp and duration of the frame.

columns.name: do_frame_id
columns.description: `slice.id` of "Choreographer#doFrame" slice.

columns.name: draw_frame_id
columns.description: `slice.id` of "DrawFrame" slice. For now, we only support the first DrawFrame slice (due to b/384322064).

columns.name: actual_frame_timeline_id
columns.description: `slice.id` from `actual_frame_timeline_slice`

columns.name: expected_frame_timeline_id
columns.description: `slice.id` from `expected_frame_timeline_slice`

columns.name: render_thread_utid
columns.description: `utid` of the render thread.

columns.description: thread id of the UI thread.

entries.name: android_frames_overrun
entries.description: The amount by which each frame missed of hit its deadline
entries.fullDescription: The amount by which each frame missed of hit its deadline. Negative if the deadline was not missed. Frames are considered janky if `overrun` is positive. Calculated as the difference between the end of the `expected_frame_timeline_slice` and `actual_frame_timeline_slice` for the frame. Availability: from S (API 31). For Googlers: more details in go/android-performance-metrics-glossary.

columns.name: frame_id
columns.description: Frame id.

columns.name: overrun
columns.description: Difference between `expected` and `actual` frame ends. Negative if frame didn't miss deadline.

entries.name: android_heap_graph_class_summary_tree
entries.description: Table containing all the Android heap graphs in the trace converted to a shortest-path tree and then aggregated by class name
entries.fullDescription: Table containing all the Android heap graphs in the trace converted to a shortest-path tree and then aggregated by class name. This table contains a "flamegraph-like" representation of the contents of the heap graph.

columns.name: graph_sample_ts
columns.description: The timestamp the heap graph was dumped at.

columns.name: upid
columns.description: The upid of the process.

columns.name: id
columns.description: The id of the node in the class tree.

columns.name: parent_id
columns.description: The parent id of the node in the class tree or NULL if this is the root.

columns.name: name
columns.description: The name of the class.

columns.name: root_type
columns.description: A string describing the type of Java root if this node is a root or NULL if this node is not a root.

columns.name: self_count
columns.description: The count of objects with the same class name and the same path to the root.

columns.name: self_size
columns.description: The size of objects with the same class name and the same path to the root.

columns.name: cumulative_count
columns.description: The sum of `self_count` of this node and all descendants of this node.

columns.name: cumulative_size
columns.description: The sum of `self_size` of this node and all descendants of this node.

entries.name: android_heap_graph_stats
entries.description: Table summarizing java heap graphs collected with the ART perfetto module
entries.fullDescription: Table summarizing java heap graphs collected with the ART perfetto module. Contains one row per heap graph, with summary statistics (e.g. total / reachable objects) and memory stats for the corresponding process at the time of the heap dump.

columns.name: upid
columns.description: The upid of the process.

columns.name: graph_sample_ts
columns.description: The timestamp the heap graph was dumped at.

columns.name: process_uptime
columns.description: The uptime of the process at the time of the heap graph dump.

columns.name: total_heap_size
columns.description: The total (reachable + unreachable) size of the Java heap in bytes.

columns.name: total_native_alloc_registry_size
columns.description: The total size of native allocations (registered with NativeAllocationRegistry) in bytes. Does *not* overlap with total_heap_size.

columns.name: total_obj_count
columns.description: The number of objects in the heap.

columns.name: reachable_heap_size
columns.description: The reachable size of the Java heap in bytes.

columns.name: reachable_native_alloc_registry_size
columns.description: The size of reachable native allocations (registered with NativeAllocationRegistry) in bytes.

columns.name: reachable_obj_count
columns.description: The number of reachable objects in the heap.

columns.name: oom_score_adj
columns.description: The OOM score adj of the process at the time of the heap graph dump.

columns.name: anon_rss_and_swap_size
columns.description: The anon RSS + swap size of the process (in bytes) at the time of the heap graph dump.

columns.name: dmabuf_rss_size
columns.description: The dmabuf size of the process (in bytes) at the time of the heap graph dump.

entries.name: android_heap_profile_summary_tree
entries.description: Table summarising the amount of memory allocated by each callstack as seen by Android native heap profiling (i.e
entries.fullDescription: Table summarising the amount of memory allocated by each callstack as seen by Android native heap profiling (i.e. profiling information collected by heapprofd). Note: this table collapses data from all processes together into a single table.

columns.name: id
columns.description: The id of the callstack. A callstack in this context is a unique set of frames up to the root.

columns.name: parent_id
columns.description: The id of the parent callstack for this callstack.

columns.name: name
columns.description: The function name of the frame for this callstack.

columns.name: mapping_name
columns.description: The name of the mapping containing the frame. This can be a native binary, library, JAR or APK.

columns.name: source_file
columns.description: The name of the file containing the function.

columns.name: line_number
columns.description: The line number in the file the function is located at.

columns.name: self_size
columns.description: The amount of memory allocated and *not freed* with this function as the leaf frame.

columns.name: cumulative_size
columns.description: The amount of memory allocated and *not freed* with this function appearing anywhere on the callstack.

columns.name: self_alloc_size
columns.description: The amount of memory allocated with this function as the leaf frame. This may include memory which was later freed.

columns.name: cumulative_alloc_size
columns.description: The amount of memory allocated with this function appearing anywhere on the callstack. This may include memory which was later freed.

entries.name: android_input_events
entries.description: All input events with round trip latency breakdown
entries.fullDescription: All input events with round trip latency breakdown. Input delivery is socket based and every input event sent from the OS needs to be ACK'ed by the app. This gives us 4 subevents to measure latencies between: 1. Input dispatch event sent from OS. 2. Input dispatch event received in app. 3. Input ACK event sent from app. 4. Input ACK event received in OS.

columns.name: dispatch_latency_dur
columns.description: Duration from input dispatch to input received.

columns.name: handling_latency_dur
columns.description: Duration from input received to input ACK sent.

columns.name: ack_latency_dur
columns.description: Duration from input ACK sent to input ACK received.

columns.name: total_latency_dur
columns.description: Duration from input dispatch to input event ACK received.

columns.name: end_to_end_latency_dur
columns.description: Duration from input read to frame present time. Null if an input event has no associated frame event.

columns.name: tid
columns.description: Tid of thread receiving the input event.

columns.name: thread_name
columns.description: Name of thread receiving the input event.

columns.name: upid
columns.description: Upid of process receiving the input event.

columns.name: pid
columns.description: Pid of process receiving the input event.

columns.name: process_name
columns.description: Name of process receiving the input event.

columns.name: event_type
columns.description: Input event type. See InputTransport.h: InputMessage#Type

columns.name: event_action
columns.description: Input event action.

columns.name: event_seq
columns.description: Input event sequence number, monotonically increasing for an event channel and pid.

columns.name: event_channel
columns.description: Input event channel name.

columns.name: normalized_event_channel
columns.description: Normalized input event channel name.

columns.name: input_event_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for the input event.

columns.name: read_time
columns.description: Timestamp input event was read by InputReader.

columns.name: dispatch_track_id
columns.description: Thread track id of input event dispatching thread.

columns.name: dispatch_ts
columns.description: Timestamp input event was dispatched.

columns.name: dispatch_dur
columns.description: Duration of input event dispatch.

columns.name: receive_track_id
columns.description: Thread track id of input event receiving thread.

columns.name: receive_ts
columns.description: Timestamp input event was received.

columns.name: receive_dur
columns.description: Duration of input event receipt.

columns.name: frame_id
columns.description: Vsync Id associated with the input. Null if an input event has no associated frame event.

columns.name: is_speculative_frame
columns.description: Indicates if the frame association was speculative rather than exact based on id match.

columns.name: event_time
columns.description: Timestamp when the input event actually occurred.

columns.name: slice_id
columns.description: Id of the CUJ slice in perfetto. Keeping the slice id column as part of this table as provision to lookup the actual CUJ slice ts and dur. The ts and dur in this table might differ from the slice duration, as they are associated with start and end frame corresponding to the CUJ.

columns.name: ts
columns.description: Start timestamp of the CUJ. Start of the CUJ as defined by the start of the first overlapping expected frame.

columns.name: ts_end
columns.description: End timestamp of the CUJ. Calculated as the end timestamp of the last actual frame overlapping with the CUJ.

columns.name: dur
columns.description: Duration of the CUJ calculated based on the ts and ts_end values.

columns.name: state
columns.description: State of the CUJ. One of "completed", "cancelled" or NULL. NULL in cases where the FT#cancel or FT#end instant event is not present for the CUJ.

columns.name: ui_thread
columns.description: thread id of the UI thread.

columns.name: layer_id
columns.description: layer id associated with the actual frame.

columns.name: begin_vsync
columns.description: vysnc id of the first frame that falls within the CUJ boundary.

columns.name: end_vsync
columns.description: vysnc id of the last frame that falls within the CUJ boundary.

entries.name: android_job_scheduler_events
entries.description: All scheduled jobs and their latencies
entries.fullDescription: All scheduled jobs and their latencies. The table is populated by ATrace using the system server ATrace category (`atrace_categories: "ss"`). You can also set the `atrace_apps` of interest. This differs from the `android_job_scheduler_states` table in the `android.job_scheduler_states` module which is populated by the `ScheduledJobStateChanged` atom. Using `android_job_scheduler_states` is preferred when the `ATOM_SCHEDULED_JOB_STATE_CHANGED` is available in the trace since it includes the constraint, screen, or charging state changes for each job in a trace.

columns.name: job_id
columns.description: Id of the scheduled job assigned by the app developer.

columns.name: uid
columns.description: Uid of the process running the scheduled job.

columns.name: package_name
columns.description: Package name of the process running the scheduled job.

columns.name: job_service_name
columns.description: Service component name of the scheduled job.

columns.name: track_id
columns.description: Thread track id of the job scheduler event slice.

columns.name: id
columns.description: Slice id of the job scheduler event slice.

columns.name: ts
columns.description: Timestamp the job was scheduled.

columns.name: dur
columns.description: Duration of the scheduled job.

entries.name: android_job_scheduler_states
entries.description: This table returns constraint changes that a job will go through in a single trace
entries.fullDescription: This table returns constraint changes that a job will go through in a single trace. Values in this table are derived from the the `ScheduledJobStateChanged` atom. This table differs from the `android_job_scheduler_with_screen_charging_states` in this module (`android.job_scheduler_states`) by only having job constraint information. See documentation for the `android_job_scheduler_with_screen_charging_states` for how tables in this module differ from `android_job_scheduler_events` table in the `android.job_scheduler` module and how to populate this table.

columns.name: id
columns.description: Unique identifier for job scheduler state.

columns.name: ts
columns.description: Timestamp of job state slice.

columns.name: dur
columns.description: Duration of job state slice.

columns.name: slice_id
columns.description: Id of the slice.

columns.name: job_name
columns.description: Name of the job (as named by the app).

columns.name: uid
columns.description: Uid associated with job.

columns.name: job_id
columns.description: Id of job (assigned by app for T- builds and system generated in U+ builds).

columns.name: package_name
columns.description: Package that the job belongs (ex: associated app).

columns.name: job_namespace
columns.description: Namespace of job.

columns.name: effective_priority
columns.description: Priority at which JobScheduler ran the job.

columns.name: has_battery_not_low_constraint
columns.description: True if app requested job should run when the device battery is not low.

columns.name: has_charging_constraint
columns.description: True if app requested job should run when the device is charging.

columns.name: has_connectivity_constraint
columns.description: True if app requested job should run when device has connectivity.

columns.name: has_content_trigger_constraint
columns.description: True if app requested job should run when there is a content trigger.

columns.name: has_deadline_constraint
columns.description: True if app requested there is a deadline by which the job should run.

columns.name: has_idle_constraint
columns.description: True if app requested job should run when device is idle.

columns.name: has_storage_not_low_constraint
columns.description: True if app requested job should run when device storage is not low.

columns.name: has_timing_delay_constraint
columns.description: True if app requested job has a timing delay.

columns.name: is_prefetch
columns.description: True if app requested job should run within hours of app launch.

columns.name: is_requested_expedited_job
columns.description: True if app requested that the job is run as an expedited job.

columns.name: is_running_as_expedited_job
columns.description: The job is run as an expedited job.

columns.name: num_previous_attempts
columns.description: Number of previous attempts at running job.

columns.name: requested_priority
columns.description: The requested priority at which the job should run.

columns.name: standby_bucket
columns.description: The job's standby bucket (one of: Active, Working Set, Frequent, Rare, Never, Restricted, Exempt).

columns.name: is_periodic
columns.description: Job should run in intervals.

columns.name: has_flex_constraint
columns.description: True if the job should run as a flex job.

columns.name: is_requested_as_user_initiated_job
columns.description: True is app has requested that a job be run as a user initiated job.

columns.name: is_running_as_user_initiated_job
columns.description: True if job is running as a user initiated job.

columns.name: deadline_ms
columns.description: Deadline that job has requested and valid if has_deadline_constraint is true.

columns.name: job_start_latency_ms
columns.description: The latency in ms between when a job is scheduled and when it actually starts.

columns.name: num_uncompleted_work_items
columns.description: Number of uncompleted job work items.

columns.name: proc_state
columns.description: Process state of the process responsible for running the job.

columns.name: internal_stop_reason
columns.description: Internal stop reason for a job.

columns.name: public_stop_reason
columns.description: Public stop reason for a job.

entries.name: android_job_scheduler_with_screen_charging_states
entries.description: This table returns the constraint, charging, and screen state changes that a job will go through in a single trace
entries.fullDescription: This table returns the constraint, charging, and screen state changes that a job will go through in a single trace. Values from this table are derived from the `ScheduledJobStateChanged` atom. This differs from the `android_job_scheduler_events` table in the `android.job_scheduler` module which is derived from ATrace the system server category (`atrace_categories: "ss"`). This also differs from the `android_job_scheduler_states` in this module (`android.job_scheduler_states`) by providing charging and screen state changes. To populate this table, enable the Statsd Tracing Config with the ATOM_SCHEDULED_JOB_STATE_CHANGED push atom id. https://perfetto.dev/docs/reference/trace-config-proto#StatsdTracingConfig This table is preferred over `android_job_scheduler_events` since it contains more information and should be used whenever `ATOM_SCHEDULED_JOB_STATE_CHANGED` is available in a trace.

Note that we only span join the duration where the lock was actually held and contended. This can be less than the duration the lock was 'waited on' when a different waiter acquired the lock earlier than the first waiter.

entries.name: android_monitor_contention_graph
entries.description: Returns a DAG of all Java lock contentions in a process
entries.fullDescription: Returns a DAG of all Java lock contentions in a process. Each node in the graph is a <thread:Java method> pair. Each edge connects from a node waiting on a lock to a node holding a lock. The weights of each node represent the cumulative wall time the node blocked other nodes connected to it.

entries.name: android_network_uptime_cost
entries.description: Compute the per-row uptime cost of network activity
entries.fullDescription: Compute the per-row uptime cost of network activity. It is common in networking to have an interface active for some time after use. For example, mobile networks are often connected for 10 or more seconds after the last packet is sent or received. This macro computes a cost factor indicating how much each row impacts the idle timer. For example, assuming a 10s timeout, the first packet will extend the timeout 10s in the future, and be assigned 10s of cost. If a packet arrives 4s later, it pushes the timer an additional 4s, receiving 4s of cost. In this simple case, cost is MIN(ts-last_packet_ts, timeout). The complication is that network events can be aggregates, with more than one packet. In such cases, we end up with a span with non-zero duration, rather than an instant, and no easy way to compute time since the last packet. The solution is to detect overlap regions and compute cost for the region as a whole. The first event in each group receives the standard uptime cost as described above. Each group has an additional cost equal to the duration of the group which is distributed using packet count as weight. For example (times in seconds, no partition, and 10 second timeout): ``` ts=5, dur=0, packet_count=1 -> group=1, uptime_cost=10 ts=7, dur=0, packet_count=1 -> group=2, uptime_cost=2 ts=20, dur=5, packet_count=9 -> group=3, uptime_cost=14.5 ts=22, dur=0, packet_count=1 -> group=3, uptime_cost=0.5 ``` The third group spans ts=20 to ts=25, with a timeout at ts=35. This gives the group a total cost of 15 which is distributed between the two rows. The 3rd row receives 10s for being first, and 9/10 the duration cost (5*9/10=4.5). The returned table schema is (id ID, uptime_cost INT64) where uptime cost is in nanoseconds.

params.name: src
params.description: A table/view/subquery containing the network events to apply the idle timeout model to. The table must contain all partition_columns, id, ts, dur, and packet_count.

params.name: partition_columns
params.description: A parenthesized set of columns to partition the analysis by.

params.name: timeout
params.description: The idle timeout, expressed in nanoseconds.

entries.name: android_network_uptime_spans
entries.description: Computes network uptime spans based on an idle timeout model
entries.fullDescription: Computes network uptime spans based on an idle timeout model. It is common in networking to have an interface active for some time after use. For example, mobile networks are often connected for 10 or more seconds after the last packet is sent or received. This macro simulates this timeout and returns spans that approximate the underlying connected regions.

entries.name: android_process_memory_intervals
entries.description: A table of process memory counter intervals, clipped to the process lifetime
entries.fullDescription: A table of process memory counter intervals, clipped to the process lifetime. Provides a comprehensive view of memory usage, including raw values and zygote-adjusted values. NOTE: For 'mem.rss.anon', 'mem.swap', 'mem.rss.file', and 'mem.heap' tracks, we subtract the average memory usage of the zygote that forked the process (its parent). This provides a better estimate of the child process's unique memory usage by accounting for the baseline memory inherited from its zygote. This is a no-op for a process whose forking zygote has no memory samples in the trace (e.g. traces with only ftrace rss_stat, where idle zygotes are not sampled; process_stats polling captures them). NOTE: Some tracks may have a spike greater than 100MiB. This can be legitimate or an accounting issue: see b/418231246 for more details.
entries.dataCheckSql: SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM process_counter_track WHERE name IN ('mem.rss.anon', 'mem.swap', 'mem.rss.file', 'mem.rss.shmem', 'Heap size (KB)') LIMIT 1) AS has_data

columns.name: id
columns.description: The id of the memory counter value

columns.name: ts
columns.description: Timestamp of the memory counter change.

columns.name: dur
columns.description: How long this memory track had this value.

columns.name: process_name
columns.description: The name of the process whose memory is being measured.

columns.name: upid
columns.description: The unique id of the process whose memory is being measured.

columns.name: pid
columns.description: The id of the process whose memory is being measured.

columns.name: memory_track_name
columns.description: The name of the memory counter track (e.g. 'mem.rss.anon').

columns.name: track_id
columns.description: The id of the memory counter track.

columns.name: value
columns.description: The value of the memory counter in bytes.

columns.name: zygote_adjusted_value
columns.description: The value of the memory counter in bytes, adjusted with the zygote's memory usage.

columns.name: track_has_spike_gt_100mib
columns.description: Whether the track has a spike greater than 100MiB.

entries.name: android_process_memory_intervals_by_oom_bucket
entries.description: Correlates memory counters with OOM adjustment scores
entries.fullDescription: Correlates memory counters with OOM adjustment scores. This table joins memory counters with OOM adjustment scores, providing insights into memory usage under system memory pressure. NOTE: For 'mem.rss.anon', 'mem.swap', 'mem.rss.file', and 'mem.heap' tracks, we subtract the average memory usage of the zygote that forked the process (its parent). This provides a better estimate of the child process's unique memory usage by accounting for the baseline memory inherited from its zygote. This is a no-op for a process whose forking zygote has no memory samples in the trace (e.g. traces with only ftrace rss_stat, where idle zygotes are not sampled; process_stats polling captures them). NOTE: Some tracks may have a spike greater than 100MiB. This can be legitimate or an accounting issue: see b/418231246 for more details.
entries.dataCheckSql: SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM process_counter_track WHERE name IN ('mem.rss.anon', 'mem.swap', 'mem.rss.file', 'mem.rss.shmem', 'Heap size (KB)') LIMIT 1) AS has_data

entries.name: android_standardize_slice_name
entries.description: Some slice names have params in them
entries.fullDescription: Some slice names have params in them. This functions removes them to make it possible to aggregate by name. Some examples are: - Lock/monitor contention slices. The name includes where the lock contention is in the code. That part is removed. - DrawFrames/ooFrame. The name also includes the frame number. - Apk/oat/dex loading: The name of the apk is removed

entries.name: android_standardize_thread_name
entries.description: Standardizes an Android thread name by extracting its core identifier to make it possible to aggregate by name
entries.fullDescription: Standardizes an Android thread name by extracting its core identifier to make it possible to aggregate by name. Removes extra parts of a thread name, like identifiers, leaving only the main prefix. Splits the name at ('-', '[', ':' , ' '). Some Examples: Given thread_name = "RenderThread-1[123]", returns "RenderThread". Given thread_name = "binder:5543_E" returns "binder". Given thread_name = "pool-3-thread-5", returns "pool". Given thread_name = "MainThread", returns "MainThread".

params.description: The full android thread name to be processed.

entries.name: android_sysui_latency_cujs

entries.fullDescription: Table tracking all latency CUJs information.

columns.name: cuj_id
columns.description: Unique incremental ID for each CUJ.

columns.name: upid
columns.description: process id.

columns.name: process_name
columns.description: process name.

columns.name: cuj_slice_name
columns.description: Name of the CUJ slice.

columns.name: cuj_name
columns.description: Name of the CUJ without the 'L<' prefix.

columns.name: slice_id
columns.description: Id of the CUJ slice in perfetto. Keeping the slice id column as part of this table as provision to lookup the actual CUJ slice ts and dur. The ts and dur in this table might differ from the slice duration, as they are associated with start and end frame corresponding to the CUJ.

columns.name: ts
columns.description: Start timestamp of the CUJ calculated as the start of the CUJ slice in trace.

columns.name: ts_end
columns.description: End timestamp of the CUJ calculated as the end timestamp of the CUJ slice.

columns.name: dur
columns.description: Duration of the CUJ calculated based on the ts and ts_end values.

columns.name: state
columns.description: State of the CUJ whether it was completed/cancelled.

entries.name: android_thread_slices_for_all_startups
entries.description: All the slices for all startups in trace
entries.fullDescription: All the slices for all startups in trace. Generally, this view should not be used. Instead, use one of the view functions related to the startup slices which are created from this table.
entries.dataCheckSql: SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM slice WHERE name IN ('bindApplication', 'activityStart', 'activityResume') LIMIT 1) AS has_data

columns.name: startup_ts
columns.description: Timestamp of startup.

columns.name: startup_ts_end
columns.description: Timestamp of startup end.

columns.name: startup_id
columns.description: Startup id.

columns.name: utid
columns.description: UTID of thread with slice.

columns.name: tid
columns.description: Tid of thread.

columns.name: thread_name
columns.description: Name of thread.

columns.name: is_main_thread
columns.description: Whether it is main thread.

columns.name: arg_set_id
columns.description: Arg set id.

columns.name: slice_id
columns.description: Slice id.

columns.name: slice_name
columns.description: Name of slice.

columns.name: slice_ts
columns.description: Timestamp of slice start.

columns.name: slice_dur
columns.description: Slice duration.

entries.name: android_user_list
entries.description: Contains information about Android users in the trace
entries.fullDescription: Contains information about Android users in the trace. This is populated by the `android.user_list` data-source which lives in traced_probes and is available on default from 26Q2+ devices. Note: `users` here corresponds to Android users *not* Linux users. So this is not about Linux uids (which in Android correspons to different apps).
entries.dataCheckSql: SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM __intrinsic_android_user_list LIMIT 1) AS has_data

columns.name: android_user_id
columns.description: The Android user id. Often useful to join with `android_process_metadata.user_id`

columns.name: type
columns.description: A string "enum" indicating the type of the user according to the system. Will be one for a few constant values e.g. HEADLESS, SECONDARY, GUEST.

entries.name: android_wakeups
entries.description: Table of parsed wakeup / suspend failure events with suspend backoff
entries.fullDescription: Table of parsed wakeup / suspend failure events with suspend backoff. Certain wakeup events may have multiple causes. When this occurs we split those causes into multiple rows in this table with the same ts and raw_wakeup values.
entries.dataCheckSql: SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM track WHERE name = 'wakeup_reason' LIMIT 1) AS has_data

columns.name: ts
columns.description: Timestamp.

columns.name: dur
columns.description: Duration for which we blame the wakeup for wakefulness. This is the suspend backoff duration if one exists, or the lesser of (5 seconds, time to next suspend event).

columns.name: raw_wakeup
columns.description: Original wakeup string from the kernel.

columns.name: on_device_attribution
columns.description: Wakeup attribution, as determined on device. May be absent.

columns.name: type
columns.description: One of 'normal' (device woke from sleep), 'abort_pending' (suspend failed due to a wakeup that was scheduled by a device during the suspend process), 'abort_last_active' (suspend failed, listing the last active device) or 'abort_other' (suspend failed for another reason).

columns.name: item
columns.description: Individual wakeup cause. Usually the name of the device that cause the wakeup, or the raw message in the 'abort_other' case.

columns.name: suspend_quality
columns.description: 'good' or 'bad'. 'bad' means failed or short such that suspend backoff is triggered.

columns.name: backoff_state
columns.description: 'new', 'continue' or NULL. Set if suspend backoff is triggered.

columns.name: backoff_reason
columns.description: 'short', 'failed' or NULL. Set if suspend backoff is triggered.

columns.name: backoff_count
columns.description: Number of times suspend backoff has occurred, or NULL. Set if suspend backoff is triggered.

columns.name: backoff_millis
columns.description: Next suspend backoff duration, or NULL. Set if suspend backoff is triggered.

entries.name: appleos_instruments_samples_summary_tree
entries.description: Table summarising the callstacks captured during all instruments samples in the trace
entries.fullDescription: Table summarising the callstacks captured during all instruments samples in the trace. Specifically, this table returns a tree containing all the callstacks seen during the trace with `self_count` equal to the number of samples with that frame as the leaf and `cumulative_count` equal to the number of samples with the frame anywhere in the tree.

columns.name: id
columns.description: The id of the callstack. A callstack in this context is a unique set of frames up to the root.

columns.name: parent_id
columns.description: The id of the parent callstack for this callstack.

columns.name: name
columns.description: The function name of the frame for this callstack.

columns.name: mapping_name
columns.description: The name of the mapping containing the frame. This can be a native binary, library, or JIT.

columns.name: source_file
columns.description: The name of the file containing the function.

columns.name: line_number
columns.description: The line number in the file the function is located at.

columns.name: self_count
columns.description: The number of samples with this function as the leaf frame.

columns.name: cumulative_count
columns.description: The number of samples with this function appearing anywhere on the callstack.

entries.name: args
entries.description: Arbitrary key-value pairs which allow adding metadata to other, strongly typed tables
entries.fullDescription: Arbitrary key-value pairs which allow adding metadata to other, strongly typed tables. Note: for a given row, only one of |int_value|, |string_value|, |real_value| will be non-null.

columns.name: id
columns.description: The id of the arg.

columns.name: arg_set_id
columns.description: The id for a single set of arguments.

columns.name: flat_key
columns.description: The "flat key" of the arg: this is the key without any array indexes.

columns.name: key
columns.description: The key for the arg.

columns.name: int_value
columns.description: The integer value of the arg.

columns.name: string_value
columns.description: The string value of the arg.

columns.name: real_value
columns.description: The double value of the arg.

columns.name: value_type
columns.description: The type of the value of the arg. Will be one of 'int', 'uint', 'string', 'real', 'pointer', 'bool' or 'json'.

columns.name: display_value
columns.description: The human-readable formatted value of the arg.

entries.name: chrome_calculate_avg_vsync_interval
entries.description: Function: compute the average Vysnc interval of the gesture (hopefully this would be either 60 FPS for the whole gesture or 90 FPS but that isnt always the case) on the given time segment
entries.fullDescription: Function: compute the average Vysnc interval of the gesture (hopefully this would be either 60 FPS for the whole gesture or 90 FPS but that isnt always the case) on the given time segment. If the trace doesnt contain the VSync TraceEvent we just fall back on assuming its 60 FPS (this is the 1.6e+7 in the COALESCE which corresponds to 16 ms or 60 FPS).
entries.returnDescription: The average vsync interval on this time segment or 1.6e+7, if trace doesn't contain the VSync TraceEvent.

entries.name: chrome_coalesced_inputs
entries.description: For each input, if it was coalesced into another input, get the other input's latency id
entries.fullDescription: For each input, if it was coalesced into another input, get the other input's latency id.

columns.name: coalesced_latency_id
columns.description: The `latency_id` of the coalesced input.

columns.name: presented_latency_id
columns.description: The `latency_id` of the other input that the current input was coalesced into. Guaranteed to be different from `coalesced_latency_id`.

entries.name: chrome_deliver_android_input_event
entries.description: DeliverInputEvent is the third step in the input pipeline
entries.fullDescription: DeliverInputEvent is the third step in the input pipeline. It is responsible for routing the input events within browser process.
entries.dataCheckSql: SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM slice WHERE name GLOB 'UnwantedInteractionBlocker::notifyMotion*' LIMIT 1) AS has_data

columns.name: utid

entries.name: chrome_event_latencies
entries.description: All EventLatency slices
entries.fullDescription: All EventLatency slices.
entries.dataCheckSql: SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM slice WHERE name = 'EventLatency' LIMIT 1) AS has_data

columns.name: id
columns.description: Slice Id for the EventLatency scroll event.

columns.name: name
columns.description: Slice name.

columns.name: ts
columns.description: The start timestamp of the scroll.

columns.name: dur
columns.description: The duration of the scroll.

columns.name: scroll_update_id
columns.description: The id of the scroll update event (aka LatencyInfo.ID).

columns.name: surface_frame_trace_id
columns.description: The id of the first frame (pre-surface aggregation) which included the scroll update and was presented. NULL if: (1) the event is not a scroll update (`event_type` is NOT GESTURE_SCROLL_UPDATE, FIRST_GESTURE_SCROLL_UPDATE, or INERTIAL_GESTURE_SCROLL_UPDATE), (2) the scroll update wasn't presented (e.g. it was an overscroll) or (3) the trace comes from an old Chrome version (https://crrev.com/c/6185817 was first included in version 134.0.6977.0 and was cherry-picked in version 133.0.6943.33).

columns.name: display_trace_id
columns.description: The id of the first frame (post-surface aggregation) which included the scroll update and was presented. NULL if: (1) the event is not a scroll update (`event_type` is NOT GESTURE_SCROLL_UPDATE, FIRST_GESTURE_SCROLL_UPDATE, or INERTIAL_GESTURE_SCROLL_UPDATE), (2) the scroll update wasn't presented (e.g. it was an overscroll) or (3) the trace comes from an old Chrome version (https://crrev.com/c/6185817 was first included in version 134.0.6977.0 and was cherry-picked in version 133.0.6943.33).

columns.name: is_presented
columns.description: Whether this input event was presented.

columns.name: event_type
columns.description: EventLatency event type.

columns.name: track_id
columns.description: Perfetto track this slice is found on.

columns.name: vsync_interval_ms
columns.description: Vsync interval (in milliseconds).

columns.name: is_janky_scrolled_frame
columns.description: Whether the corresponding frame is janky based on the Event.ScrollJank.DelayedFramesPercentage.FixedWindow metric.

columns.name: buffer_available_timestamp
columns.description: Timestamp of the BufferAvailableToBufferReady substage.

columns.name: buffer_ready_timestamp
columns.description: Timestamp of the BufferReadyToLatch substage.

columns.name: latch_timestamp
columns.description: Timestamp of the LatchToSwapEnd substage (or LatchToPresentation as a fallback).

columns.name: swap_end_timestamp
columns.description: Timestamp of the SwapEndToPresentationCompositorFrame substage.

columns.name: presentation_timestamp
columns.description: Frame presentation timestamp aka the timestamp of the SwapEndToPresentationCompositorFrame substage. TODO(b/341047059): temporarily use LatchToSwapEnd as a workaround if SwapEndToPresentationCompositorFrame is missing due to b/247542163.

entries.name: chrome_get_v3_jank_cause_id
entries.description: Given two slice Ids A and B, find the maximum difference between the durations of it's direct children with matching names for example if slice A has children named (X, Y, Z) with durations of (10, 10, 5) and slice B has children named (X, Y) with durations of (9, 9), the function will return the slice id of the slice named Z that is A's child, as no matching slice named Z was found under B, making 5 - 0 = 5 the maximum delta between both slice's direct children
entries.fullDescription: Given two slice Ids A and B, find the maximum difference between the durations of it's direct children with matching names for example if slice A has children named (X, Y, Z) with durations of (10, 10, 5) and slice B has children named (X, Y) with durations of (9, 9), the function will return the slice id of the slice named Z that is A's child, as no matching slice named Z was found under B, making 5 - 0 = 5 the maximum delta between both slice's direct children
entries.returnDescription: The slice id of the breakdown that has the maximum duration delta.

params.name: janky_slice_id
params.description: The slice id of the parent slice that we want to cause among it's children.

params.name: prev_slice_id
params.description: The slice id of the parent slice that's the reference in comparison to |janky_slice_id|.

entries.name: chrome_jetstream_3_measure
entries.description: Final list of JetStream 3 measures
entries.fullDescription: Final list of JetStream 3 measures. Classified into: * 'First' (Iteration 0), * 'Average' (Iteration 1..N) * 'Worst' (slowest iterations). Note: 'Average' includes all iterations except the first and the worst ones. In the actual benchmark scoring, 'Average' includes 'Worst', but the measure table avoids listing the same slice twice. For WSL, the subtest name is the slice name (e.g., 'WSL-stdlib').

entries.name: chrome_scroll_frame_info_v4
entries.description: A list of all presented Chrome frames which contain scroll updates together with additional metadata, pipeline stages breakdown and jank classification according to the scroll model of Chrome's scroll jank v4 metric
entries.fullDescription: A list of all presented Chrome frames which contain scroll updates together with additional metadata, pipeline stages breakdown and jank classification according to the scroll model of Chrome's scroll jank v4 metric.

columns.name: id
columns.description: Slice ID of the 'ScrollJankV4' slice, which represents a frame presented by Chrome that contains at least one scroll update. Can be joined with `chrome_scroll_jank_v4_results.id`.

columns.name: scroll_update_latency_id
columns.description: Id of the `LatencyInfo.Flow` slices corresponding to the first scroll update in this frame. Can be joined with `chrome_gesture_scroll_updates.id`, `chrome_scroll_update_info.id`, `chrome_scroll_update_refs.scroll_update_latency_id` and many other tables.

columns.name: scroll_id
columns.description: Id of the scroll this frame belongs to. Can be joined with `chrome_event_latency.scroll_id`.

columns.name: vsync_interval_dur
columns.description: The VSync interval that this frame was produced for according to the BeginFrameArgs.

columns.name: is_janky
columns.description: Whether this frame is janky based on the `Event.ScrollJank.DelayedFramesPercentage4.FixedWindow` metric.

columns.name: is_inertial
columns.description: Whether the corresponding scroll is inertial (fling).

columns.name: frame_index_in_scroll
columns.description: The position of the frame in a scroll (starting from 1).

columns.name: real_abs_total_raw_delta_pixels
columns.description: The absolute total raw (unpredicted) delta of all real scroll updates included in this frame (in pixels). NULL if the first scroll update in this frame is synthetic.

columns.name: browser_uptime_dur
columns.description: Duration from the start of the browser process to the input generation timestamp of the first scroll update in this frame. NULL if the first scroll update in this frame is synthetic.

columns.name: first_input_generation_ts
columns.description: Input generation timestamp (from the Android system) for the first scroll update in this frame. NULL if the first scroll update in this frame is synthetic.

columns.name: input_reader_dur
columns.description: Duration from the generation timestamp to the end of InputReader's work. Only populated when atrace 'input' category is enabled. NULL if the first scroll update is synthetic.

columns.name: input_dispatcher_dur
columns.description: Duration of InputDispatcher's work. Only populated when atrace 'input' category is enabled. NULL if the first scroll update in this frame is synthetic.

columns.name: previous_last_input_to_first_input_generation_dur
columns.description: Duration from the previous input (last input that wasn't part of this frame) to the first input in this frame.

columns.name: browser_utid
columns.description: Utid for the browser main thread, on which Chrome received the first scroll update in this frame. NULL if the first scroll update in this frame is synthetic.

columns.name: first_input_generation_to_browser_main_dur
columns.description: Duration from input generation to when the browser received the first scroll update in this frame. NULL if the first scroll update in this frame is synthetic.

columns.name: first_input_generation_to_browser_main_delta_dur
columns.description: Difference between `first_input_generation_to_browser_main_dur` for this frame and the previous frame in the same scroll. NULL if the first scroll update in either frame is synthetic.

columns.name: first_input_touch_move_received_slice_id
columns.description: Slice id for the `STEP_SEND_INPUT_EVENT_UI` slice for the `TouchMove` event associated with the first scroll update in this frame. NULL if the first scroll update in this frame is synthetic.

columns.name: first_input_touch_move_received_ts
columns.description: Timestamp for the `STEP_SEND_INPUT_EVENT_UI` slice for the `TouchMove` event associated with the first scroll update in this frame. NULL if the first scroll update in this frame is synthetic.

columns.name: first_input_touch_move_processing_dur
columns.description: Duration for processing the `TouchMove` event associated with the first scroll update in this frame. NULL if the first scroll update in this frame is synthetic.

columns.name: first_input_touch_move_processing_delta_dur
columns.description: Difference between `first_input_touch_move_processing_dur` for this frame and the previous frame in the same scroll. NULL if the first scroll update in either frame is synthetic.

columns.name: first_input_scroll_update_created_slice_id
columns.description: Slice id for the `STEP_SEND_INPUT_EVENT_UI` slice which created the `GestureScrollUpdate` event from the `TouchMove` event for the first scroll update in this frame. NULL if the first scroll update in this frame is synthetic.

columns.name: first_input_scroll_update_created_ts
columns.description: Timestamp for the `STEP_SEND_INPUT_EVENT_UI` slice which created the `GestureScrollUpdate` event from the `TouchMove` event for the first scroll update in this frame. NULL if the first scroll update in this frame is synthetic.

columns.name: first_input_scroll_update_processing_dur
columns.description: Duration for creating the `GestureScrollUpdate` event from the `TouchMove` event for the first scroll update in this frame. NULL if the first scroll update in this frame is synthetic.

columns.name: first_input_scroll_update_processing_delta_dur
columns.description: Difference between `first_input_scroll_update_processing_dur` for this frame and the previous frame in the same scroll. NULL if the first scroll update in either frame is synthetic.

columns.name: first_input_scroll_update_created_end_ts
columns.description: End timestamp for the `STEP_SEND_INPUT_EVENT_UI` slice which created the `GestureScrollUpdate` event from the `TouchMove` event for the first scroll update in this frame. NULL if the first scroll update in this frame is synthetic.

entries.name: chrome_scroll_input_deltas
entries.description: The raw input deltas for all input events which were part of a scroll
entries.fullDescription: The raw input deltas for all input events which were part of a scroll.

columns.name: scroll_update_id
columns.description: Scroll update id (aka LatencyInfo.ID) for this scroll update input event.

columns.name: delta_x
columns.description: The delta in pixels (scaled to the device's screen size) how much this input event moved over the X axis vs previous, as reported by the OS.

columns.name: delta_y
columns.description: The delta in pixels (scaled to the device's screen size) how much this input event moved over the Y axis vs previous, as reported by the OS.

entries.name: chrome_scroll_input_offsets
entries.description: The raw coordinates and pixel offsets for all input events which were part of a scroll
entries.fullDescription: The raw coordinates and pixel offsets for all input events which were part of a scroll.

columns.name: scroll_update_id
columns.description: An ID for this scroll update (aka LatencyInfo.ID).

columns.name: scroll_id
columns.description: An ID for the scroll this scroll update belongs to.

columns.name: ts
columns.description: Timestamp the of the scroll input event.

columns.name: delta_y
columns.description: The delta in raw coordinates between this scroll update event and the previous.

columns.name: relative_offset_y
columns.description: The total delta of all scroll updates within the same as scroll up to and including this scroll update.

entries.name: chrome_scroll_jank_cause_descriptions
entries.description: Source of truth of the descriptions of EventLatency-based scroll jank causes
entries.fullDescription: Source of truth of the descriptions of EventLatency-based scroll jank causes.

columns.name: event_latency_stage
columns.description: The name of the EventLatency stage.

columns.name: cause_process
columns.description: The process where the cause of scroll jank occurred.

columns.name: cause_thread
columns.description: The thread where the cause of scroll jank occurred.

columns.name: cause_description
columns.description: A description of the cause of scroll jank.

entries.name: chrome_scroll_jank_causes_with_event_latencies
entries.description: Combined description of scroll jank cause and associated event latency stage
entries.fullDescription: Combined description of scroll jank cause and associated event latency stage.

columns.name: name
columns.description: The name of the EventLatency stage.

columns.name: description
columns.description: Description of the EventLatency stage.

columns.name: cause_process
columns.description: The process name that may cause scroll jank.

columns.name: cause_thread
columns.description: The thread name that may cause scroll jank. The thread will be on the cause_process.

columns.name: cause_description
columns.description: Description of the cause of scroll jank on this process and thread.

entries.name: chrome_scroll_jank_intervals_v3
entries.description: Defines slices for all of janky scrolling intervals in a trace
entries.fullDescription: Defines slices for all of janky scrolling intervals in a trace.

columns.name: id
columns.description: The unique identifier of the janky interval.

columns.name: ts
columns.description: The start timestamp of the janky interval.

columns.name: dur
columns.description: The duration of the janky interval.

entries.name: chrome_scroll_jank_tags
entries.description: List of scroll jank causes that apply to scroll frames which are janky according to Chrome's v1 scroll jank metric
entries.fullDescription: List of scroll jank causes that apply to scroll frames which are janky according to Chrome's v1 scroll jank metric. Contains zero or more tags for each janky frame.

columns.name: frame_id
columns.description: Frame ID.

columns.name: tag
columns.description: Tag of the scroll jank cause.

entries.name: chrome_scroll_jank_tags_v4
entries.description: List of scroll jank causes that apply to scroll frames which are janky according to Chrome's v4 scroll jank metric
entries.fullDescription: List of scroll jank causes that apply to scroll frames which are janky according to Chrome's v4 scroll jank metric. Contains zero or more tags for each janky frame.

columns.name: frame_id
columns.description: Frame ID. Can be joined with `chrome_scroll_frame_info_v4.id`.

columns.name: tag
columns.description: Tag of the scroll jank cause.

entries.name: chrome_scroll_jank_v4_reasons
entries.description: Reasons why the Scroll Jank V4 metric marked frames as janky
entries.fullDescription: Reasons why the Scroll Jank V4 metric marked frames as janky. A frame might be janky for multiple reasons, so this table might contain multiple rows with the same `id` and distinct `jank_reason`s. Available since Chrome 145.0.7573.0 and cherry-picked into 144.0.7559.31.

columns.name: id
columns.description: Slice ID of the 'ScrollJankV4' slice. Can be joined with `chrome_scroll_jank_v4_results.id`.

columns.name: jank_reason
columns.description: A reason why the frame is janky. Possible values: * 'MISSED_VSYNC_DUE_TO_DECELERATING_INPUT_FRAME_DELIVERY': Chrome's input→frame delivery slowed down to the point that it missed one or more VSyncs. * 'MISSED_VSYNC_DURING_FAST_SCROLL': Chrome missed one or more VSyncs in the middle of a fast regular scroll. * 'MISSED_VSYNC_AT_START_OF_FLING': Chrome missed one or more VSyncs during the transition from a fast regular scroll to a fling. * 'MISSED_VSYNC_DURING_FLING': Chrome missed one or more VSyncs in the middle of a fling.

columns.name: missed_vsyncs
columns.description: Number of VSyncs that that Chrome missed (for `jank_reason`) before presenting the first scroll update in the frame. Greater than zero.

entries.name: chrome_scroll_jank_v4_results
entries.description: Results of the Scroll Jank V4 metric for frames which contain one or more scroll updates
entries.fullDescription: Results of the Scroll Jank V4 metric for frames which contain one or more scroll updates. See https://docs.google.com/document/d/1AaBvTIf8i-c-WTKkjaL4vyhQMkSdynxo3XEiwpofdeA and `EventLatency.ScrollJankV4Result` in https://source.chromium.org/chromium/chromium/src/+/main:base/tracing/protos/chrome_track_event.proto for more information. Available since Chrome 145.0.7573.0 and cherry-picked into 144.0.7559.31.

columns.name: id
columns.description: Slice ID of the 'ScrollJankV4' slice.

columns.name: name
columns.description: Slice name ('ScrollJankV4').

columns.name: ts
columns.description: The timestamp at the start of the slice.

columns.name: dur
columns.description: The duration of the slice.

columns.name: result_id
columns.description: ID of this frame's result.

columns.name: is_janky
columns.description: Whether this frame is janky. True if and only if there's at least one row with `id` in `chrome_scroll_jank_v4_reasons`. If true, then `vsyncs_since_previous_frame` must be greater than one.

columns.name: vsyncs_since_previous_frame
columns.description: How many VSyncs were between (A) this frame and (B) the previous frame. If this value is greater than one, then Chrome potentially missed one or more VSyncs (i.e. might have been able to present this scroll update earlier). NULL if this frame is the first frame in a scroll.

columns.name: running_delivery_cutoff
columns.description: The running delivery cut-off based on frames preceding this frame. NULL if ANY of the following holds: * This frame is the first frame in a scroll. * All frames since the beginning of the scroll up to and including the previous frame have been non-damaging. * The most recent janky frame was non-damaging and all frames since then up to and including the previous frame have been non-damaging.

columns.name: adjusted_delivery_cutoff
columns.description: The running delivery cut-off adjusted for this frame. NULL if ANY of the following holds: * This frame is the first frame in a scroll. * This frame is non-damaging. * All frames since the beginning of the scroll up to and including the previous frame have been non-damaging. * The most recent janky frame was non-damaging and all frames since then up to and including the previous frame have been non-damaging. * `vsyncs_since_previous_frame` is equal to one.

columns.name: current_delivery_cutoff
columns.description: The delivery cut-off of this frame. NULL if this frame is non-damaging.

columns.name: real_first_event_latency_id
columns.description: Trace ID of the first real scroll update included in this frame. Can be joined with `chrome_event_latencies.scroll_update_id`. NULL if this frame contains no real scroll updates.

columns.name: real_first_input_generation_ts
columns.description: The actual generation timestamp of the first real scroll update included (coalesced) in this frame. NULL if this frame contains no real scroll updates.

columns.name: real_last_input_generation_ts
columns.description: The actual generation timestamp of the last real scroll update included (coalesced) in this frame. NULL if this frame contains no real scroll updates.

columns.name: real_abs_total_raw_delta_pixels
columns.description: The absolute total raw (unpredicted) delta of all real scroll updates included in this frame (in pixels). NULL if this frame contains no real scroll updates.

columns.name: real_max_abs_inertial_raw_delta_pixels
columns.description: The maximum absolute raw (unpredicted) delta out of all inertial (fling) scroll updates included in this frame (in pixels). NULL if there were no inertial scroll updates in this frame.

columns.name: synthetic_first_event_latency_id
columns.description: Trace ID of the first synthetic scroll update included in this frame. Can be joined with `chrome_event_latencies.scroll_update_id`. NULL if this frame contains no synthetic scroll updates.

columns.name: synthetic_first_extrapolated_input_generation_ts
columns.description: The generation timestamp of the first synthetic scroll update included (coalesced) in this frame extrapolated based on the input generation → begin frame duration of the most recent real scroll update. NULL if ANY of the following holds: * This frame contains no synthetic scroll updates. * This frame is janky (i.e. `is_janky` is true). * All frames since the beginning of the scroll up to and including the previous frame have contained only synthetic scroll updates. * The most recent janky frame contained only synthetic scroll updates and all frames since then up to and including the previous frame have contained only synthetic scroll updates.

columns.name: synthetic_first_original_begin_frame_ts
columns.description: The begin frame timestamp of the first synthetic scroll update included (coalesced) in this frame. NULL if this frame contains no synthetic scroll updates. If not NULL, it's less than or equal to `begin_frame_ts`.

columns.name: first_scroll_update_type
columns.description: Type of the first scroll update in this frame. Possible values: * 'REAL' * 'SYNTHETIC_WITH_EXTRAPOLATED_INPUT_GENERATION_TIMESTAMP' * 'SYNTHETIC_WITHOUT_EXTRAPOLATED_INPUT_GENERATION_TIMESTAMP' The first scroll update is decided as follows: * For real scroll updates, we consider their actual input generation timestamp. * For synthetic scroll updates, we extrapolate their input generation timestamp based on the input generation → begin frame duration of the most recent real scroll update UNLESS ANY of the following holds (in which case we DON'T extrapolate input generation timestamps for synthetic scroll updates in this frame): * This frame is janky. * All frames since the beginning of the scroll up to and including the previous frame have contained only synthetic scroll updates. * The most recent janky frame contained only synthetic scroll updates and all frames since then up to and including the previous frame have contained only synthetic scroll updates. If, based on the above rules, the scroll update with the earliest input generation timestamp is a real scroll update, then this frame's type is 'REAL'. If the scroll update with the earliest input generation timestamp is a synthetic scroll update, then this frame's type is 'SYNTHETIC_WITH_EXTRAPOLATED_INPUT_GENERATION_TIMESTAMP'. If this frame contains only synthetic scroll updates but it wasn't possible to extrapolate their input generation timestamp (for any of the reasons listed above), then this frame's type is 'SYNTHETIC_WITHOUT_EXTRAPOLATED_INPUT_GENERATION_TIMESTAMP'.

columns.name: first_event_latency_id
columns.description: Trace ID of the first scroll update included in this frame. * If `first_scroll_update_type` is 'REAL', then `first_event_latency_id` is equal to `real_first_event_latency_id`. * If `first_scroll_update_type` is 'SYNTHETIC_WITH_EXTRAPOLATED_INPUT_GENERATION_TIMESTAMP' or 'SYNTHETIC_WITHOUT_EXTRAPOLATED_INPUT_GENERATION_TIMESTAMP', then `first_event_latency_id` equal to `synthetic_first_event_latency_id`. Can be joined with `chrome_event_latencies.scroll_update_id`.

columns.name: damage_type
columns.description: Type of scroll damage in this frame. Possible values: * 'DAMAGING' * 'NON_DAMAGING_WITH_EXTRAPOLATED_PRESENTATION_TIMESTAMP' * 'NON_DAMAGING_WITHOUT_EXTRAPOLATED_PRESENTATION_TIMESTAMP' A frame F is non-damaging if the following conditions are BOTH true: 1. All scroll updates in F are non-damaging. A scroll update is non-damaging if it didn't cause a frame update and/or didn't change the scroll offset. 2. All frames between (both ends exclusive): a. the last frame presented by Chrome before F and b. F are non-damaging. If this frame is damaging, its type is 'DAMAGING'. If this frame is non-damaging and its presentation timestamp could be extrapolated based on the begin frame → presentation duration of the most recent damaging frame, its type is 'NON_DAMAGING_WITH_EXTRAPOLATED_PRESENTATION_TIMESTAMP'. If this frame is non-damaging and its presentation timestamp couldn't be extrapolated for ANY of the reasons below, its type is 'NON_DAMAGING_WITHOUT_EXTRAPOLATED_PRESENTATION_TIMESTAMP': * This frame is janky and non-damaging. * All frames since the beginning of the scroll up to and including this frame have been non-damaging. * The most recent janky frame was non-damaging and all frames since then up to and including the this frame have been non-damaging. Note: The `first_scroll_update_type` and `damage_type` columns are orthogonal. The former depends on whether the frame is synthetic (only contains synthetic scroll updates). The latter depends on whether the frame is damaging. For example: * If a frame is synthetic and damaging, it will[1] have an extrapolated input generation timestamp. * If a frame is real and non-damaging, it will[1] have an extrapolated presentation timestamp. * If a frame is both synthetic and damaging, it will[1] have both timestamps extrapolated. [1] As long as there's Chrome past performance to extrapolate based on.

columns.name: vsync_interval
columns.description: The VSync interval that this frame was produced for according to the BeginFrameArgs.

columns.name: begin_frame_ts
columns.description: The begin frame timestamp, at which this frame started, according to the BeginFrameArgs. In older Chrome versions, this column is incorrectly NULL for frames whose `damage_type` is 'NON_DAMAGING_WITHOUT_EXTRAPOLATED_PRESENTATION_TIMESTAMP'.

columns.name: presentation_ts
columns.description: The presentation timestamp of the frame. * If `damage_type` is 'DAMAGING', then `presentation_ts` is the actual presentation timestamp. * If `damage_type` is 'NON_DAMAGING_WITH_EXTRAPOLATED_PRESENTATION_TIMESTAMP', then `presentation_ts` is an extrapolated timestamp based on the begin frame → presentation duration of the most recent damaging frame. * If `damage_type` is 'NON_DAMAGING_WITHOUT_EXTRAPOLATED_PRESENTATION_TIMESTAMP', then `presentation_ts` is NULL. In older Chrome versions, this column is incorrectly always NULL.

entries.name: chrome_scroll_presented_deltas
entries.description: The page offset delta (by how much the page was scrolled vs previous frame) for each frame
entries.fullDescription: The page offset delta (by how much the page was scrolled vs previous frame) for each frame. This is the resulting delta that is shown to the user after the input has been processed. `chrome_scroll_input_deltas` tracks the underlying signal deltas between consecutive input events.

columns.name: scroll_update_id
columns.description: Scroll update id (aka LatencyInfo.ID) for this scroll update input event.

columns.name: delta_x
columns.description: The delta in pixels (scaled to the device's screen size) how much this input event moved over the X axis vs previous, as reported by the OS.

columns.name: delta_y
columns.description: The delta in pixels (scaled to the device's screen size) how much this input event moved over the Y axis vs previous, as reported by the OS.

columns.name: offset_x
columns.description: The page offset in pixels (scaled to the device's screen size) along the X axis.

columns.name: offset_y
columns.description: The page offset in pixels (scaled to the device's screen size) along the Y axis.

entries.name: chrome_scroll_stats

entries.fullDescription: Scroll jank frame presentation stats for individual scrolls.

columns.name: janky_frame_count

columns.name: janky_frame_percent
columns.description: The % of frames that janked in the scroll.

entries.name: chrome_scroll_update_info
entries.description: Timestamps and durations for the critical path stages during scrolling
entries.fullDescription: Timestamps and durations for the critical path stages during scrolling. This table covers both the input-associated (before coalescing inputs into a frame) and frame-associated (after coalescing inputs into a frame) stages of a scroll: ... | +--------------+--------------+ | | V V +-------------------------+ +-------------------------+ | _scroll_update_INPUT_ | | _scroll_update_FRAME_ | | timestamps_and_metadata | | timestamps_and_metadata | +------------+------------+ +------------+------------+ | | V V +-----------------------+ +-----------------------+ | chrome_scroll_update_ | | chrome_scroll_update_ | | INPUT_pipeline | | FRAME_pipeline | +-----------+-----------+ +-----------+-----------+ | | +--------------+--------------+ | V +---------------------------+ | chrome_scroll_update_info | +---------------------------+

columns.name: id
columns.description: Id of the `LatencyInfo.Flow` slices corresponding to this scroll event.

columns.name: scroll_id
columns.description: Id of the scroll this scroll update belongs to.

columns.name: previous_input_id
columns.description: Id (`LatencyInfo.ID`) of the previous input in this scroll.

columns.name: frame_display_id
columns.description: Id (`display_trace_id`) of the aggregated frame which this scroll update was presented in.

columns.name: vsync_interval_ms
columns.description: Vsync interval (in milliseconds).

columns.name: is_presented
columns.description: Whether this input event was presented.

columns.name: is_janky
columns.description: Whether the corresponding frame is janky based on the Event.ScrollJank.DelayedFramesPercentage.FixedWindow metric. This comes directly from `perfetto.protos.EventLatency.is_janky_scrolled_frame`.

columns.name: is_inertial
columns.description: Whether the corresponding scroll is inertial (fling). If this is `true`, "generation" and "touch_move" related timestamps and durations will be null.

columns.name: is_first_scroll_update_in_scroll
columns.description: Whether this is the first update in a scroll. First scroll update can never be janky.

columns.name: is_first_scroll_update_in_frame
columns.description: Whether this is the first input that was presented in the frame.

columns.name: browser_uptime_dur
columns.description: Duration from the start of the browser process to the first input generation timestamp.

columns.name: generation_ts
columns.description: Input generation timestamp (from the Android system).

columns.name: input_reader_dur
columns.description: Duration from the generation timestamp to the end of InputReader's work. Only populated when atrace 'input' category is enabled.

columns.name: input_dispatcher_dur
columns.description: Duration of InputDispatcher's work. Only populated when atrace 'input' category is enabled.

columns.name: since_previous_generation_dur
columns.description: Duration from the generation timestamp for the previous input to this input's generation timestamp.

columns.name: generation_to_browser_main_dur
columns.description: Duration from input generation to when the browser received the input.

columns.name: browser_utid
columns.description: Utid for the browser main thread.

columns.name: touch_move_received_ts

columns.name: touch_move_processing_dur
columns.description: Duration for processing a `TouchMove` event.

columns.name: scroll_update_created_slice_id
columns.description: Slice id for the `STEP_SEND_INPUT_EVENT_UI` slice for the gesture scroll.

columns.name: scroll_update_created_ts
columns.description: Timestamp for the `STEP_SEND_INPUT_EVENT_UI` slice for the gesture scroll.

columns.name: scroll_update_processing_dur
columns.description: Duration for creating a `GestureScrollUpdate` from a `TouchMove` event.

columns.name: scroll_update_created_end_ts
columns.description: End timestamp for the `STEP_SEND_INPUT_EVENT_UI` slice for the above.

entries.fullDescription: Chrome web content interactions (InteractionToFirstPaint), including associated high-level metrics and properties. Multiple events may occur for the same interaction; each row in this table represents the primary (longest) event for the interaction. Web content interactions are discrete, as opposed to sustained (e.g. scrolling); and only occur with the web content itself, as opposed to other parts of Chrome (e.g. omnibox). Interaction events include taps, clicks, keyboard input (typing), and drags.
